title: Domain Errors, State, and Resource Lifecycles · Changed-Input Lab
summary: Keep loan state transitions, storage sessions, and notification failures recoverable and observable. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a stateful failure-resilient service with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model allowed states and transitions explicitly so every method preserves domain invariants.
Define a small domain error hierarchy that communicates recoverable business failures without leaking infrastructure details.
Translate storage, network, and parsing failures at ownership boundaries while preserving causes for operators.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Transaction Boundaries applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Group state changes into atomic application operations with clear success, rollback, and idempotency behavior.
